Latest — 22 July 2026

The UAE Merged Three AI Regulators Into One. Most Companies Won't Merge Two.

Mid-week check-in, UAE first: the US eases AI chip export controls for the UAE, a new Federal AI and Data Authority starts sharpening enforcement, and the silo data says adoption hasn't slowed — it's just getting more fragmented. Plus fresh developments across the GCC, US, Europe and China.

Wednesday AI Insights

The World Starts Writing AI's Rulebook

UAE eases chip export rules and merges AI policy under one federal authority. Saudi's HUMAIN partners with Cohere and xAI. OpenAI pitches Washington a 5% equity stake. The EU's Cloud and AI Development Act publishes as AI Act deadlines slip. Only 8% of organisations have real AI governance.
